notes: First person singular pronoun, doubling as the possessive 'my' (Sranan Tongo pronouns are not case-marked). Confirmed identically in Koen Kamphuijs's grammar page (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranagra.html, 'Persoonlijke voornaamwoorden' + 'Het bezittelijk voornaamwoord is hetzelfde als het persoonlijk voornaamwoord') and suriname.nu's grammar page (https://www.suriname.nu/201cult/sranan01.html, listing 'me'/'mi' for both 'ik' and 'mijn').

Second person singular pronoun and possessive 'your'. Confirmed identically in Kamphuijs's and suriname.nu's grammar pages. Already attested in this repo via the greeting phrases 'Fa waka?'/'Fa yu tan?' (see vocab/greetings.json), which this entry now generalises as its own vocab item.

Third person singular: 'a' is the subject form, 'en' the object form (used after a verb or preposition), e.g. 'A naki en' ('hij sloeg hem'). Confirmed in both Kamphuijs's grammar page (explicit subject/object rule with example) and suriname.nu's grammar page (which lists 'en' for 'hij, zij, het' without the subject/object distinction — Kamphuijs's fuller account is preferred here, corroborated rather than contradicted by suriname.nu).
First person plural pronoun and possessive 'our'. Confirmed as 'wi' in both Kamphuijs's and suriname.nu's grammar pages (suriname.nu also gives the alternate spelling 'we').

Second person plural pronoun. suriname.nu's grammar page gives 'unu' cleanly for 'jullie'. Kamphuijs's grammar page's pronoun table lists 'yu' (not 'unu') for 'jullie', which looks like a copy-paste slip from the 'jij' row directly above it — everywhere else in this repo's sourcing (Wikipedia's Sranan Tongo grammar sketch, creole-linguistics descriptions) the second-person-plural form is 'unu', matching suriname.nu, so 'yu' for 'jullie' is treated as an error on Kamphuijs's page rather than a genuine variant.

Third person plural pronoun and possessive 'their'. Confirmed identically in Kamphuijs's and suriname.nu's grammar pages. Note 'den' is also the plural definite article ('den bromki' — 'de bloemen', per Kamphuijs's article table) — same form, different grammatical function; the article sense is not yet authored as its own vocab entry (deferred to unit-03-srn-nouns).
Demonstrative 'this', placed after the noun it modifies (e.g. 'a lampu disi' — 'this lamp'). Confirmed identically in Kamphuijs's and suriname.nu's grammar pages.

Demonstrative 'that', referring to something farther away than 'disi', placed after the noun it modifies (e.g. 'a sani dati' — 'that thing'). Confirmed identically in Kamphuijs's and suriname.nu's grammar pages.
Question word 'what'. Confirmed identically in Kamphuijs's and suriname.nu's grammar pages.
Question word 'who'. Confirmed identically in Kamphuijs's and suriname.nu's grammar pages.

Question word 'where'. Confirmed identically in Kamphuijs's and suriname.nu's grammar pages. Kamphuijs's page also lists the alternate form 'Ope?', not corroborated by suriname.nu — 'Pe' is used here as the cross-confirmed form.
Question word 'which'. Confirmed identically in Kamphuijs's and suriname.nu's grammar pages.

Question word 'when'. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's grammar page (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranagra.html) — single-sourced (not present on suriname.nu's grammar page), not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.
Question word 'how', used with verbs (e.g. 'Fa yu tan?' — already in this repo's greetings vocab). Confirmed identically in Kamphuijs's and suriname.nu's grammar pages.

Question word 'how much/many'. Source: Koen Kamphuijs's grammar page (http://koenkamphuijs.nl/sranantongo/sranagra.html) — single-sourced (not present on suriname.nu's grammar page), not yet cross-checked against a second independent source.

Question word 'why'. Kamphuijs's grammar page gives 'Fu san ede' (with the pronunciation note 'fsayde'); suriname.nu's grammar page gives 'Fu sa e de' for the same meaning. These are likely the same phrase transcribed differently rather than genuine variants, but that hasn't been confirmed against a third source, so this ships as `needs-verification` with Kamphuijs's more detailed (pronunciation-annotated) spelling preferred over suriname.nu's.
